title: Below floor — escalation
description: Buyer cap (₹345) is mathematically below the seller's hard minimum acceptable price (margin ₹350 + buffer ₹5 = ₹355). No matter how many rounds run, the math cannot bridge. Both agents act in good faith; the deal is structurally infeasible. Cleaner escalation demo than 'tight-budget-escalation' because the failure is unambiguous and tied to a single named constraint (SELLER_CONFIG.marginPrice + minProfitMargin), not to the treasury floor.

expectedOutcome.likely: 3 rounds run, seller rejects in final round (buyer's offer ≤ ₹345 < seller's minAcceptable ₹355). Both agents write escalation audit JSON + .txt report. Useful for regulator-facing demos of the audit chain when the math doesn't allow a deal.
expectedOutcome.possible: If LLM generously interprets seller intent and proposes ACCEPT below ₹355, applySellerConstraints overrides it to REJECT or COUNTER at ₹355. Final result still escalation.
expectedOutcome.failureMode: Escalation IS the success criterion here — the demo shows the agents refusing to close an infeasible deal, with the audit explaining why. Outcome 'escalation' in audit JSON, gap ≥ ₹10 reported.

declaredButDeferred: sellerIntent (entirely — seller acts on its own .env config)
